evals.prompt: /update-project --project test-project --session-summary "Today I decided to switch from random splitting to scaffold-aware splitting. Random split is invalid because Tanimoto analysis showed 15% near-duplicates that would leak between train and test sets. I'll use the Bemis-Murcko scaffold approach."
evals.expected_output: decisions-log.md updated with a new entry for today's date capturing the scaffold-aware splitting decision and the rationale about 15% near-duplicates. open-questions.md should close the open question about splitting strategy. No results-log or methods.md changes expected. Diff shown before writing.

assertions.name: decision_added_to_decisions_log
assertions.description: decisions-log.md contains a new entry with scaffold-aware splitting and the near-duplicate rationale

assertions.name: open_question_closed
assertions.description: open-questions.md shows splitting question as [CLOSED] with an answer

assertions.name: diff_shown_before_write
assertions.description: Skill shows a diff preview and asks for confirmation before writing

assertions.name: no_results_or_methods_changes
assertions.description: results-log.md and methods.md are not modified (no results or methods in this session)

evals.prompt: /update-project --project test-project --session-summary "Ran sensitivity analysis with 5 model variants today. All showed 1-3% accuracy — the sqrt-prop global architecture is broken at all tested hyperparameter settings. Best variant: learning rate 1e-4, hidden dim 256, gave 3.1% accuracy. This is a dead end."
evals.expected_output: results-log.md updated with a new entry capturing: run name, numerical results (1-3% accuracy, best 3.1% at lr=1e-4 hidden=256), interpretation (dead end). dashboard status line updated. No decision or methods changes expected.

assertions.name: results_entry_with_numbers
assertions.description: results-log.md contains new entry with numerical values (1-3% accuracy, 3.1%, lr=1e-4)

assertions.name: interpretation_present
assertions.description: Results entry includes interpretation that this architecture is not viable

assertions.name: dashboard_updated
assertions.description: project-dashboard.md status line updated to reflect sensitivity analysis outcome

assertions.name: no_false_decision
assertions.description: decisions-log.md not modified with a spurious entry (abandoning sqrt-prop may be inferred but wasn't explicitly stated as a decision)

evals.prompt: /update-project --project test-project --session-summary "Started using CASTEP today for geometry optimisations. Used GGA-PBE functional, 830 eV plane-wave cutoff, 1x1x1 k-grid, norm-conserving pseudopotentials, no dispersion correction. This is the main DFT setup going forward."
evals.expected_output: methods.md updated with a new table row for CASTEP with the specified parameters. decisions-log.md may get an entry about choosing CASTEP as the DFT code. No results or questions changes expected.

assertions.name: methods_row_added
assertions.description: methods.md contains a new table row with CASTEP, GGA-PBE, 830 eV cutoff

assertions.name: parameters_complete
assertions.description: Methods row includes k-grid (1x1x1) and pseudopotential type

assertions.name: not_duplicate
assertions.description: Existing methods table rows are not duplicated

evals.prompt: /update-project --project test-project --session-summary "Big day: ran the dedup pipeline and got 155,286 molecules after removing conformers and near-duplicates (started with 163k). Decided to use MD5 hashes (32-char) instead of SHA256 modulo for compound IDs — more reliable. Also realised we need to validate the dedup against a known dataset before publishing. New open question: what reference set to use for dedup validation? Concept worth noting: the idea of using scaffold diversity as a quality metric for ML datasets."
evals.expected_output: All four files updated correctly: results-log.md gets dedup result (155,286 molecules), decisions-log.md gets MD5 vs SHA256 decision, open-questions.md gets new open question about validation reference set, dashboard updated. Galaxy candidate about scaffold diversity noted but not written.

assertions.name: results_logged_with_numbers
assertions.description: results-log.md has entry with 155,286 molecules and context about starting from 163k

assertions.name: decision_logged
assertions.description: decisions-log.md has entry about MD5 hash choice with rationale

assertions.name: new_question_added
assertions.description: open-questions.md has new [OPEN] entry about dedup validation reference set

assertions.name: galaxy_candidate_listed_not_written
assertions.description: Skill lists scaffold diversity as a Galaxy candidate but does not create any note file

assertions.name: no_cross_contamination
assertions.description: Decisions appear in decisions-log.md only, results in results-log.md only — no mixing of content between files
